title: My Accounts Page Label and Layout Refinements
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want the account management page to use clear, consistent terminology and formatting, so that I can navigate settings and manage my account without confusion.
description:
UX refinements to the My Accounts page for consistent naming conventions and typography:

**Left Navigation Changes:**
- "Asset Type" renamed to "Settings"
- "Settings" link renamed to "My Settings"
- "My Account" renamed to "Manage Account"
- Left navigation links made consistent using the same heading level

**Header Changes:**
- Title changed from "My Accounts" to "Manage My Accounts"

**Typography:**
- Subtitles changed from H4 to H5 for consistency
- H2 section heading changed to H5

**Terminology:**
- "My Tenant(s)" renamed to "Guest Users" since "tenant" is an internal term not suitable for customer-facing UI

A typo ("My Accoun" missing the 't') was also identified and fixed.
acceptance_criteria: Left navigation shows 'Settings', 'My Settings', and 'Manage Account'
All subtitles use H5 for consistency
Left navigation link formatting is consistent
ux_changes: Renamed navigation labels and page titles for clarity. Standardized heading levels to H5. Replaced internal terminology ('Tenant') with user-friendly label ('Guest Users'). Fixed typo in 'My Account' label.
implementation_notes: Multiple iterative changes based on review feedback. The layout was also restructured to show account information, invite option, and accepted members in a cleaner format.
comment_summary: Developer implemented all label changes and layout updates. Product identified a typo and inconsistent heading levels during review, both of which were quickly fixed. Overall page layout was praised.
